attacking_style: Attacks from long range (3-8km to go) with sustained grinding power rather than explosive acceleration, often from breakaways or on penultimate climbs. Excels at converting positional advantage into solo victories through superior descending.
team_tactics: Astana sets controlled tempo on climbs rather than attacking for him; has shown willingness to defend yellow aggressively. Team struggles with echelon/tactical protection in flat stages. Often positioned as dual-threat with other leaders (Martin, Aru).
terrain_preference: Mid-mountain classics and stage races with selective climbs followed by technical descents. Thrives on sustained gradients where grinding power dominates over explosive accelerations. Less suited to ultra-steep finale walls requiring punch.
vulnerability: Lacks ultimate sprint/acceleration to close out wins from small groups (multiple 2nd places in 2019). Astana's poor echelon positioning exposes him in windy flat stages. Injury-prone in Grand Tours (crashes in 2017, 2019, 2020).
key_patterns: Peaks at Tour preparation races (Dauphiné, Suisse, Romandie wins) rather than Tour itself
Attacks 3-8km from finish with sustained power, not explosive accelerations (4+ victories)
Uses descending skill to extend gaps after climbing attacks (Tirreno, Romandia, Lombardia)
Loses sprints/acceleration contests from small elite groups despite strong positioning (Strade Bianche, Fleche)
Grand Tour campaigns compromised by crashes and poor team support in tactical situations
Grinding, big-gear climbing style over punchy accelerations on sustained gradients
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 Tour de Suisse showed better-than-expected form with late attacks, but no data after mid-2022. Peak years 2018-2020 showed consistent podiums but Grand Tour struggles
